steps.title: Pin down what to look up
steps.content: The sentence asks for the depth at which the highest number of tools made from beach-collected clamshells was found. So scan the "beach-collected" column of the table for its maximum and report the matching depth row.

steps.title: Find the maximum in the beach-collected column
steps.content: Reading down the beach-collected counts across the depth rows, the largest value occurs at the 3–4 meters row (the count there far exceeds the counts at the other depths).

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 5–6 meters below the surface.: not the maximum row — the beach-collected count here is lower than at 3–4 m. ✗
  • Choice B — 4–5 meters below the surface.: also lower than the 3–4 m row — misreads the peak. ✗
  • Choice C — 3–4 meters below the surface.: the row containing the largest beach-collected value. ✓
  • Choice D — 6–7 meters below the surface.: one of the smallest counts in the column — clearly not the maximum. ✗
That is the correct answer.
